Hudson Global ( (STRR) ) has shared an announcement.

MVB Financial ( (MVBF) ) has provided an update.

MVB Financial Corp. released an updated investor presentation for use in investor meetings, outlining its evolution from a community bank into a differentiated fintech-focused institution with $3.3 billion in assets, $2.8 billion in deposits and a growing fintech banking platform as of year-end 2025. The materials highlight strong deposit growth across payments, Banking-as-a-Service and gaming, an expanding advanced-stage fintech pipeline with multiple product launches through early 2026, and management’s view that its scalable model, strong capital and liquidity, and improving core profitability create room for further growth and potential valuation re-rating versus traditional bank and fintech peers.

The presentation underscores that CoRe banking remains the funding and compliance backbone while the fintech side provides fee income and margin expansion, supported by an experienced leadership team with more than 20 years’ average industry experience. For stakeholders, the deck signals MVB’s strategic commitment to scaling its fintech sponsorship, payments and AI-enabled initiatives, leveraging a diversified deposit base and proven innovation track record, including the Victor Technologies transaction, to reinforce its niche as a “banker of choice” to fintechs and to capture a larger share of the total addressable market.

The most recent analyst rating on (MVBF) stock is a Hold with a $27.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on MVB Financial stock, see the MVBF Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MVBF Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MVBF is a Neutral.

Technicals are a clear near-term headwind with bearish momentum and the stock trading below key moving averages. To see Spark’s full report on MVBF stock, click here.

More about MVB Financial

MVB Financial Corp. is an innovative, tech-forward bank that powers solutions for leading fintech companies nationwide, combining a traditional commercial and retail lending and deposit franchise with a high-growth fintech platform. Its offerings span payment services, Banking-as-a-Service, and gaming-related deposit and payment solutions for more than 40 clients, including major brands such as DraftKings, FanDuel and BetMGM, supported by a robust compliance and risk management infrastructure.

The company operates a diversified model in which its CoRe banking business provides a stable funding base and regulatory expertise, while its fintech platform drives growth and margin expansion across payments, BaaS and gaming. MVB has grown total assets to $3.3 billion and total deposits to $2.8 billion as of 2025, with significant non-interest-bearing deposits and a nationwide fintech market focus underpinning its positioning as a scaled fintech banking specialist.

An update from Immix Biopharma ( (IMMX) ) is now available.

On March 25, 2026, Immix Biopharma amended its at-the-market offering agreement with Citizens JMP Securities, allowing the company to sell up to $100 million of its common stock from time to time through the firm as sales agent under an existing Form S-3 shelf registration. The stock may be sold in ordinary at-the-market transactions, as well as privately negotiated and block trades, subject to limits tied to registered share capacity, authorized but unissued shares, and Form S-3 eligibility thresholds.

The structure provides Immix Biopharma with a flexible mechanism to raise additional equity capital over time, potentially supporting ongoing corporate and clinical initiatives while diluting existing shareholders as new shares are issued into the market.

P3 Health Partners ( (PIII) ) has issued an update.

On March 19, 2026, P3 Health Partners entered into a long-term Statement of Work and related Master Services Agreement with a large nonprofit health insurer in Nebraska to support primary care providers in the insurer’s Medicare Advantage network. Under the deal, P3 will deliver clinical, operational and data-driven services through 2030, initially earning management services fees in 2026 and 2027, with a shift to a global risk financial arrangement beginning in 2028.

The agreements include multiple termination and performance provisions, including the client’s ability to exit if P3 fails to meet agreed metrics in 2026 or if key personnel leave, and an automatic SOW termination with a capped break-up fee if the insurer does not pursue a Medicare Advantage bid for 2027–2028. P3 has also committed to provide transition support if services are terminated, underscoring both the strategic importance and operational complexity of the partnership for its Medicare Advantage-focused business.

data.title: CIMG Reports Strong Growth and Expands Bitcoin Reserves
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

CIMG ( (CIMG) ) just unveiled an announcement.

CIMG Inc., a Hong Kong-based digital health and sales development group, is pursuing a dual-track strategy in health consumer goods and computing power technology products across Asia, underpinned by AI-driven marketing and a cryptocurrency-focused growth model. The company serves a roster of consumer brands and holds substantial Bitcoin reserves, aligning its business trajectory with both digital health trends and the volatile digital-asset market.

On March 21, 2026, CIMG amended a previously disclosed February 2026 Regulation S private placement with non-U.S. investors, cancelling a planned second closing while modifying existing convertible notes and warrants to reflect its shift from Nasdaq to OTC trading. The amendments introduced a $0.10 per-share conversion floor, redefined the trading market to include OTC venues and set a cash-only warrant exercise price of $0.015 per share, while the company committed to register resale of the underlying shares.

In a March 25, 2026 release of results for the three months ended December 31, 2025, CIMG reported total revenue of $15.77 million, up sharply from $22,853 a year earlier, driven by early-stage commercialization of medicine-food homology products and new computing power solutions. The company expanded its digital asset position to 730 Bitcoins with a carrying value of about $64 million, reported book value of roughly $3.60 per share and highlighted that crypto-market volatility could materially sway future results.

During the quarter ended December 31, 2025, CIMG advanced its strategic transformation by launching GPU hardware with embedded AI data-processing modules and securing enterprise computing power contracts, including with China Merchants Bank. It also added wholly owned subsidiaries in Shenzhen and Foshan and acquired Braincon Limited and its Beijing affiliate to strengthen production, sales and R&D capabilities and deepen localized operations in the Asia market.

Chairman and CEO Alice Wang said the company aims to scale online and offline channels for maca and medicine-food homology products and to improve gross margins through pricing and cost discipline, while boosting R&D investment in computing power products to broaden its enterprise customer base. She added that CIMG is pushing follow-on financing to optimize its capital structure, appealing Nasdaq’s delisting decision and tightening internal financial management and controls as it seeks to shift from business transformation to sustained profit growth.

The most recent analyst rating on (CIMG) stock is a Sell with a $0.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on CIMG stock, see the CIMG Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CIMG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CIMG is a Underperform.

CIMG’s overall stock score is low due to significant financial challenges and poor valuation metrics. The technical analysis indicates a bearish trend, further impacting the score. While there are some positive corporate developments, they are not enough to offset the financial and compliance issues.

To see Spark’s full report on CIMG stock, click here.

More about CIMG

CIMG Inc. is a Hong Kong-based business group specializing in digital health and sales development, with a cryptocurrency-focused strategy that leverages AI and digital assets such as Bitcoin and stablecoins to drive client growth. The company focuses on health consumer brands and utilizes digital marketing and computing power technology products to enhance sales and brand value, primarily in the Asia market.

Its client portfolio includes brands such as Kangduoyuan, Maca-Noni, Qianmao, Huomao and Coco-mango, and it is expanding into medicine-food homology products and GPU-based computing power solutions for enterprise customers. CIMG also actively manages significant Bitcoin holdings, which introduce exposure to digital-asset price volatility into its balance sheet and financial performance.

Average Trading Volume: 1,011,997

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: $3.25M

data.title: Corcept Wins FDA Approval for Lifyorli Ovarian Cancer Therapy
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Corcept Therapeutics ( (CORT) ).

On March 25, 2026, Corcept Therapeutics announced U.S. FDA approval of Lifyorli (relacorilant) in combination with nab-paclitaxel for adults with platinum-resistant epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube or primary peritoneal cancer previously treated with one to three regimens including bevacizumab. Lifyorli, the first FDA-approved selective glucocorticoid receptor antagonist, is an oral therapy that enhances chemotherapy sensitivity by counteracting cortisol’s suppression of apoptosis.

The approval rests on the pivotal Phase 3 ROSELLA trial, which enrolled 381 patients and showed a 35 percent reduction in risk of death and a 30 percent reduction in disease progression versus nab-paclitaxel alone, delivering a 4.1-month improvement in median overall survival. Investigators reported the combination was generally well tolerated with manageable safety risks such as neutropenia, infections and adrenal insufficiency, and leading oncologists and patient advocates indicated the regimen could become a new standard of care for this hard-to-treat population.

ROSELLA’s global enrollment across North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific and Latin America, along with prior data presentations at ASCO 2025 and publication in The Lancet, underscores the therapy’s clinical significance and international relevance. Corcept is also pursuing European commercialization, leveraging relacorilant’s orphan drug designation and a submitted marketing application to the EMA, positioning the company to expand its oncology footprint in a large, underserved market of women with platinum-resistant ovarian cancer.

The most recent analyst rating on (CORT) stock is a Buy with a $60.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Corcept Therapeutics stock, see the CORT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CORT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CORT is a Neutral.

The score is supported by strong financial quality (rapid revenue growth, very high gross margins, and a low-debt balance sheet) and a generally constructive earnings outlook with meaningful pipeline catalysts. It is constrained by clearly bearish technicals (price far below key moving averages with negative MACD), premium valuation (P/E ~41.7), and elevated regulatory/legal risks highlighted on the earnings call (CRL and patent ruling) alongside 2025 margin and cash-flow softening.

To see Spark’s full report on CORT stock, click here.

More about Corcept Therapeutics

Corcept Therapeutics, based in Redwood City, Calif., is a commercial-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on discovering and developing medications that treat severe endocrinologic, oncologic, metabolic and neurologic disorders by modulating the effects of cortisol. The company is advancing its proprietary selective glucocorticoid receptor antagonist relacorilant across multiple cancers, including ovarian, endometrial, cervical, pancreatic and prostate tumors.

Average Trading Volume: 2,292,519

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $3.58B

For a thorough assessment of CORT stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

On March 25, 2026, Nanobiotix issued a statement addressing recent media speculation about a potential public takeover of the company. data.title: GH Research Publishes Phase 2b GH001 TRD Data and Highlights Severity-Independent Efficacy
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An announcement from GH Research ( (GHRS) ) is now available.

On March 25, 2026, GH Research PLC reported that full results from its randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled Phase 2b trial of mebufotenin (GH001) in treatment-resistant depression have been published and peer-reviewed in JAMA Psychiatry. The article details primary and secondary efficacy outcomes, safety and tolerability, and six‑month open‑label extension data, providing a key validation milestone for the program.

The company also disclosed that a supporting manuscript has been accepted for publication in Psychopharmacology Bulletin, showing GH001’s efficacy does not diminish with increasing numbers of prior antidepressant treatment failures. This severity‑independent effect contrasts with the well-known decline in remission rates seen in legacy trials such as STAR*D, reinforcing GH001’s potential to address heavily pretreated TRD patients and underpinning GH Research’s push toward global pivotal trials.

The most recent analyst rating on (GHRS) stock is a Hold with a $13.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on GH Research stock, see the GHRS Stock Forecast page.

Ramin Kamfar with a board that is 20% female and 80% ethnically diverse. The filing details the company’s board committees, director compensation, equity incentive capacity, related-party management and DST program fee flows, and significant insider and 5% shareholder ownership, giving investors clearer visibility into how management is paid, how capital is allocated through affiliates, and how governance and compliance structures operated through year-end 2025.

It also disclosed extensive stock ownership guidelines, anti-hedging and pledging controls, a clawback policy for incentive fees, and full compliance with Section 16 filings, while confirming Grant Thornton LLP’s continued role as auditor with $674,000 in 2025 audit fees and no incentive fees paid to the manager for 2024–2025.

Aegon ( (AEG) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 25, 2026, Aegon announced the nomination of Marco Keim, CEO of its International business and Executive Committee member, to succeed Aegon CEO Lard Friese as a non-independent member of the Supervisory Board of Dutch insurer a.s.r., in which Aegon holds a roughly 24% strategic stake. The change follows Aegon’s December 10, 2025 disclosure that Friese would step down from the a.s.r. board to focus on relocating Aegon’s head office and legal seat to the U.S., with Keim’s appointment to be voted on at a.s.r.’s AGM on May 20, 2026 and, if approved, running until July 4, 2028.

The nomination underscores Aegon’s intent to maintain strong oversight and influence over its sizeable a.s.r. holding while freeing Friese to concentrate on the complex U.S. relocation, a key strategic shift for the group. Keim’s extensive leadership background in Dutch insurance, including his tenure as CEO of Aegon the Netherlands and prior role at Zwitserleven, positions him to safeguard Aegon’s interests at a.s.r., providing continuity for shareholders and signaling continued commitment to the Dutch market despite the corporate redomiciling.

data.title: Honda and Sony Halt AFEELA EV Launches as Honda Overhauls Electrification Strategy

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Honda Motor Company ( (HMC) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On March 25, 2026, Honda said its equity-method affiliate Sony Honda Mobility will discontinue development and launch of its first EV model AFEELA1 and a second planned model, prompting Sony, Honda and the JV to conduct a broad review of the venture’s business direction. The companies plan to reassess Sony Honda Mobility’s role and positioning in light of a changing EV market, although Honda expects any impact on its revised full-year earnings forecast for the year ending March 31, 2026 to be immaterial.

The March 25 announcement follows Honda’s March 12, 2026 decision to cancel certain EV models slated for North American production, as part of a wider reassessment of its automobile electrification strategy. In response to slowing EV demand in North America, shifting U.S. policy, and intensifying software-driven competition in China and Asia, Honda now expects to book substantial impairment and related losses on EV-focused assets and some equity-method investments in China for the fiscal year ending March 31, 2026.

data.title: Tower Semiconductor, Nuvoton to Restructure TPSCo Fabs in Japan
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

An update from Tower ( (TSEM) ) is now available.

On March 25, 2026, Tower Semiconductor and Nuvoton Technology Corporation Japan agreed to a strategic restructuring of their joint venture Tower Partners Semiconductor Co. (TPSCo), which operates 12‑inch and 8‑inch fabs in Japan. Under the framework agreement, Tower will assume full ownership and operational control of TPSCo’s 12‑inch fabrication facility and related foundry business in Uozu, while TPSCo’s 8‑inch fab in Tonami will stay within TPSCo, which will become a wholly owned Nuvoton subsidiary.

As part of the deal, Nuvoton’s Japanese unit will pay Tower $25 million upon closing, and both sides plan to maintain uninterrupted customer engagements, development programs and employee stability at the two sites. The realignment, expected to close on April 1, 2027 subject to regulatory and customary approvals, is designed to sharpen each company’s strategic focus and bolster their competitiveness in the global semiconductor market by better aligning assets with long‑term business goals and supporting mutual production services between the facilities.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSEM) stock is a Buy with a $180.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Tower stock, see the TSEM Stock Forecast page.

More about Tower

The company offers customizable process technologies including SiGe, BiCMOS, mixed-signal/RF CMOS, CMOS image sensors, power management, photonics, and MEMS, supported by design enablement and process transfer services across fabs in Israel, the U.S., Japan and a shared facility in Italy.
